<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm ready to write a blog post about why the Inter font is loved.

The post will cover their exclusive Inter-only setup on jorap.com, self-hosting, and a weight-and-tracking hierarchy. It will highlight practical benefits like small-size readability and variable weights, mention alternatives they considered, and honestly note Inter's lack of distinctive personality.

The post will follow a one-page-zine or extension-cord style. It will be written in JoRap's plain, direct voice without em dashes, currency, or AI-speak. Draft status will be marked with a `__` prefix. Categories and tags will include Website, Design, and Typography. Related design notes may be pulled in.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Relevant design principles to explore include digital minimalism, minimum effective dose, and people-centered design.

Drafting the blog post now. Title options include "Why I Picked Inter for Everything." The structure will open with a hook about font shopping fatigue, then explain Inter's screen-friendly design, and conclude with the author's decision to use it exclusively.

The draft will cover honest tradeoffs, such as giving up distinctive personality and serif pairings. It will describe jorap.com’s exact setup: 16px body text, weights 400 through 700, self-hosted woff2 files, and a system font fallback. Hierarchy will rely on weight and tracking alone, not font mixing. The post will close with advice for a friend starting a site. Avoided writing includes em dashes, flowery terms like "delve," and technical jargon without clear purpose.
</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Draft is at `content/english/blog/__inter-font.md` (`draft: true`).

**Angle:** Not a font review. It's why you stopped font-shopping and settled on Inter as the only family on jorap.com.

**Sections:**
- Familiarity beats novelty for long reads
- Clean at 16px body (your actual size)
- One-family hierarchy via weight/tracking, not pairing
- Screen-first without the system-ui OS lottery
- Self-hosted woff2 at build time (privacy, reliability)
- Honest tradeoffs (won't carry a fashion-brand site)
- When you'd still pick something else

Rewrote the opening around your actual path:

- **Discovery via *Refactoring UI*** — you weren't font-shopping; you were learning UI and Inter showed up as a practical default
- **"What pulled me in first"** now ties back to the book (readable while fixing buttons/cards/forms), not generic ubiquity

New opener:

```19:23:content/english/blog/__inter-font.md
I didn't go looking for a font. I found **Inter** in [*Refactoring UI*](https://www.refactoringui.com/) - Adam Wathan and Steve Schoger's book on making interfaces look good without a design degree.

I was reading it for spacing, color, and hierarchy. The type section pointed at Inter as a solid screen default. I dropped it into a side project, then the next one, then this site. I wasn't comparing twenty specimens. I was trying to ship something that didn't look like a Bootstrap leftover.

It stuck. Inter is the only font family on [jorap.com](/) now - headings, body, nav, buttons, all of it.
```

Anything else in the draft that doesn't match your experience?
